content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN) and a non-negative integer xxx. The nodes of the tree have non-negative integer weights; let's denote the weight of node iii by aia_iai​.

Next, let's define the XOR value of a tree as the bitwise XOR of weights of all its nodes.

Chef wants to remove zero or more edges from his tree in such a way that each individual tree in the forest formed by removing these edges has XOR value xxx. Help him compute the number of ways to remove a set of edges such that this condition is satisfied. Since the answer may be large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and xxx.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
Each of the following N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv in Chef's tree.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ― the number of ways to remove edges, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are five valid ways to remove edges, splitting the tree into subtrees on nodes:

[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6] and [7] by removing the edge (3-7)
[1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7] and [5] by removing the edge (2-5)
[2, 4, 5] and [1, 3, 6, 7] by removing the edge (1-2)
[2, 4, 5], [1], [3,6] and [7] by removing edges (1-2), (1-3) and (3-7)
[1, 2, 4], [5], [3,6] and [7] by removing edges (2-5), (1-3) and (3-7)
